blockers: The fallback extracts before classifying entries, so tar hard links become ordinary files and are accepted rather than explicitly rejected at the archive trust boundary.
overall_reasoning: The agent investigated both GitHub attestation and archive code, asked the consequential all-files-versus-partial-coverage question, preserved archive-blob verification, and made missing or invalid per-file attestations terminal. The repaired semantic verifier confirms that this is a connected alternative design rather than dead code, but also identifies a real trust-boundary gap: post-extraction WalkDir classification cannot distinguish hard-link members from regular files. The patch is therefore not safe to merge. The tests validate digesting and basic extraction but do not exercise the per-file decision end to end or cover hard links and other archive entry types. The review does not override the deterministic failure.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The archive-first and per-file flow is readable and documented, but it adds several wrappers and synchronous whole-file reads where a tighter archive-aware abstraction would be easier to audit.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The patch reuses the existing GitHub bridge and extraction machinery, but supports only tar-family archives and performs security classification after extraction rather than at the archive-entry layer.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: Changes remain focused on sigstore, the GitHub backend, archive extraction, and associated tests, without unrelated refactoring.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Missing, invalid, and errored file attestations fail closed, but hard-link entries are not rejected before extraction and can be treated as ordinary covered files.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Digest and tar extraction tests are useful, but the HTTP test only asserts connection failure and the suite omits hard links, symlinks, duplicate paths, and an end-to-end all-members attestation decision.

strengths: Asked a precise trust-policy clarification and implemented the selected all-regular-files coverage rule rather than guessing permissively.
Preserved archive-level attestation verification and limited fallback to the explicit no-attestations outcome.
Made missing or invalid per-file attestations terminal instead of silently downgrading.
summary: Sound investigation and clarification with a connected fail-closed fallback, but not mergeable because hard-link archive members are accepted after extraction and focused security tests are incomplete.
